chief_complaint: A 45-year-old woman presented with electrocardiographic abnormalities after a suicide attempt with partial hanging.
history: The patient survived a suicide attempt with partial hanging and presented with electrocardiographic (ECG) abnormalities that mimicked acute coronary syndrome (ACS). She exhibited ST-segment abnormalities and QT interval prolongation, all of which resolved within 1 month.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG showed ST-segment abnormalities and QT interval prolongation. Other investigations were not mentioned.
